rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Deft For Requests For Admissions-Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Defendant's Requests For Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER AND PROPER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT VARGAS' REQUEST OF ADMISSION - SET TWO ADDRESSED TO ALL PLAINTIFFS IS GRANTED. THE PAPERS REFLECT THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE PROVIDED NO RESPONSE AS REQUIRED BY LAW TO THE REQUESTS AND HAS UNILATERALLY OBJECTED TO RESPOND ON THE BASIS OF ALLEGED FAILURE BY DEFENDANT VARGAS TO SATISFY HIS DISCOVERY RESPONSIBILITIES. PLAINTIFFS INDIVIDUALLY WILL FILE FULL AND APPROPRIATE RESPONSES NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF THIS ORDER; FAILURE TO DO WILL RESULT IN THE ADMISSION OF THE FACTS ASSERTED IN THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AND PLAINTIFFS WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE TO CONTRADICT THE FACTS ASSERTED THEREIN. SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR COUNSEL IN THE SUM OF $1.185.00 WHICH WILL BE PAID TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT ON JANUARY 8, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JULIS VARGAS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT VARGAS' FIRST REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ADDRESSED TO ALL PLAINTIFFS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ARE ORDERED TO FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES AND PRODUCE ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTION NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFFS HAVE PROVIDED NO RESPONSES OR DOCUMENTS AND THE OBJECTIONS ASSERTED FOR THEIR FAILURE TO DO SO ARE WITHOUT MERIT. PARTIES DO NOT HAVE A UNILATERAL RIGHT TO REFUSE TO RESPOND TO APPROPRIATE DISCOVERY REQUESTS MERELY BECAUSE THEY BELIEVE THAT THE PROPOUNDING PARTY HAS NOT BEEN SUFFICIENTLY FORTHCOMING IN ITS DISCOVERY RESPONSES. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,185.00 WILL BE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR ATTORNEY WITH PAYMENT TO BE MADE WITHIN 10 FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND PRESENT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 8, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN ALUY et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA N.A & EQUITABLE DEED COMPANY's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CROSS-COMPLAINTS' BANK OF AMERICA AND EQUITABLE DEED COMPANY MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-DEFENDANT MOUSAW TO MAKE FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NUMBERS 3, 6, 7-27 AND FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH CROSS-DEFENDANT ASSERTED UNMERITORIOUS OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, CROSS-DEFENDANT DENIED EACH OF THEM AND IN HIS RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 JUSTIFIED THE DENIAL ON THE GROUNDS THAT HE DID NOT HAVE SUFFICIENT RECOLLECTION TO ENABLE HIM TO ADMIT THE ACCURACY OF THE REQUESTED ADMISSION. WHILE ORDINARILY SUCH A BASIS FOR REFUSING TO ADMIT GENERALLY ELICITS A RESPONSE THAT THE PARTY IS UNABLE TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY, THERE IS NOTHING THAT COMPELS A PARTY TO ACCEPT THE ACCURACY OF A DOCUMENT PREPARED BY THE OPPOSING PARTY AND ADMIT THE FACTS ASSERTED ABSENT AN INDEPENDENT RECOLLECTION OF THE FACTS. HOWEVER, BY FAILING TO ADMIT FACTS WHICH A PARTY IS NOT IN A POSITION TO IN GOOD FAITH CONTEST AND REQUIRING PROOF AT TRIAL, THE PARTY RUNS THE RISK OF SANCTIONS BEING IMPOSED BY REASON OF ITS FAILURE TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE REQUESTED ADMISSIONS. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AT THIS TIME. COUNSEL FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR CROSS-COMPLAINTS AND SUBMIT THE ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 8, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel 1) Written Discovery Responses From Deft 2) To Have Reqeusts For Admissions Deemed Admitted; 3) To Compel Defts Appearance At Deposition; And 4) For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 4, PLAINTIFFs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO & PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel 1) Written Discovery Responses From Defendant 2) To Have Reqeusts For Admissions Deemed Admitted 3) To Compel Defendant's Appearance At Deposition And 4) For Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO FILE SUCH RESPONSES THE GENUINESS OF DOCUMENT WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND THE FACTS ASSERTED IN REQUESTS NUMBERS 1-22 SHALL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WILL BE PRECLUDED FOR INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE TO THE CONTRARY. rulings.Case Title: JERUSALEM CHURCH OF GOD IN CHRIST, A CALIFORNIA VS. TIMOTHY JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JERUSALEM CHURCH OF GOD IN CHRIST, A CALIFORNIA RELIGIOUS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. Although Defendants did not file a separate statement in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(b)(3), Plaintiff has not met its initial burden of proof for summary adjudication. Plaintiff's citations to the COGIC Constitution do not support its assertion that COGIC's Constitution authorizes Bishops and Pastors to terminate and remove members. (UMF nos. 6, 14; see Evidence, Exhibit A ("Lyles Decl."), p. 3:24 - 4:9 and Exhibit A, Constitution, p. A-8 - A-9, A-21, Exhibit B ("A. Galbraith Decl."), p. 2:19-21, 3:9-24.) Constitution, Part I, Article III, Section D, 2, 8 and Section D, p. 85 do not confer this purported authority. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE SHU PUN CHAN TRUST VS. 2400 TELEGRAPH PARTNERSHIP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JOHN CHAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE SHU PUN CHAN TRUST'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Woodson as follows: Denied. No outstanding discovery issues were presented that would require an order of the Court. Defendant 2400 Telegraph Partnership has produced substantive amended discovery responses to Plaintiff, including 2400+ pages of documents that are in part comprised of two years' worth of documents not requested by Plaintiff in the first set of discovery. Sanctions are denied. New counsel diligently responded to outstanding discovery requests. Sufficient cause was not shown to warrant sanctions against the former counsel. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LEE VS. MOROSO CONSTRUCTION INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Moroso Construction Inc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, PLAINTIFFs CHARLES LEE & ALEXANDRA BOYLE's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Moroso Construction Inc. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS' AND CROSS- DEFENDANTS', CHARLES LEE AND ALEXANDRA BOYLE, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 34 IS GRANTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT MOROSO CONSTRUCTION, INC. CONTENDS THAT IT HAS PRODUCED ALL SUCH DOCUMENTS IN ITS POSSESSION IT WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS A LISTING BY BATES NUMBERS REFLECTING THAT RESPONSE; TO THE EXTENT THAT IT IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ON THE BASIS OF PRIVILEGE, IT WILL PROVIDE A LISTING OF EACH SUCH DOCUMENT AND DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF THE BASIS FOR SUCH CLAIM. PLAINTIFFS' AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 40-44 IS DENIED. THE BURDEN IMPOSED ON DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINT BY REQUIRING IT TO PRODUCE ALL CONSTRUCTION CONTRACTS ENTERED INTO BY IT FOR A PERIOD OF 10 YEARS FAR OUTWEIGHS THE LIKELIHOOD THAT SUCH PRODUCTION WILL PRODUCE EVIDENCE RELEVANT TO THIS CONTRACTUAL DISPUTE. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY IS REQUESTED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND TO SUBMIT SUCH PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 8, 2013. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. GEORGE REDFEARN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Response To Discovery, Order For The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Asserted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 6, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's Motion To Compel Response To Discovery, Order For The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Asserted. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THERE APPEARS TO BE NO OPPOSITION FILED ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT GEORGE REDFEARN; ACCORDINGLY THE COURT WILL ISSUE THE FOLLOWING ORDER: (1) DEFENDANT GEORGE REDFEARN WILL FILE NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FULL RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO EACH INTERROGATORY SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; (2) DEFENDANT GEORGE REDFEARN WILL PRODUCE NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER ALL DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR IN PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR INSPECTION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; (3) DEFENDANT GEORGE REDFEARN WILL PROVIDE WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER FULL RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION. TO THE EXTENT THAT HE FAILS TO DO SO THE GENUINESS OF THE DOCUMENTS AND THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS ASSERTED IN THE REQUEST WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT GEORGE REDFEARN WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM OFFERING AT TRIAL ANY EVIDENCE SEEKING TO CONTRADICT SUCH ADMISSIONS. (4) PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000 AGAINST DEFENDANT GEORGE REDFEARN AND HIS COUNSEL. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHOULD PREPARE AN ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT SUCH PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT ON JANUARY 8, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DERRICK RICHARDS WHITE VS. U.C.S.F. MEDICAL CENTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The demurrer to the first cause of action for "general negligence" is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs cannot state a negligence cause of action on behalf of their daughter, and there appears to be no reasonable likelihood that Plaintiffs can amend to cure the defects that appear in the Second Amended Complaint as to "general negligence." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PLACE2PARK LLC, et al VS. CITY AND C0UNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTERED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs have failed to exhaust their administrative remedies prior to filing this suit. See Complaint 18-32. Code Civ. Proc. §430.10 (a). Plaintiffs' suit is barred as a matter of law by the "pay first, litigate later" rule. See Batt v. City and County of San Francisco, (2008) 155 Cal. App. 4th 65; Flying Dutchman Park, Inc. v. City and County of San Francisco, (2001) 93 Cal. App. 4th 1129. Plaintiffs do not identify how they will amend the complaint to cure these defects. The court grants the requests for judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CLIVE LIGHTWOOD VS. DIRECTV LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT DIRECTV LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. The allegations of the complaint sufficiently identify the date and general vicinity of the assault/battery/trespass. Defendant can reasonably respond to the pleading. Discovery will reveal further detail and the Court liberally construes the complaint per CCP 452. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
